title: Aegis Cross-Host Capability Matrix
description: Schema for manifest/capabilities.json — the single source of truth for cross-host capability status. Consumed by docs/harnesses.md, docs/capability-matrix.md, adapter projection.md host-status tables, and the projector lints. Hand-edits to files derived from capabilities.json are validator failures.

schemaVersion.description: Schema revision. Bump on any breaking shape change.

capabilities.description: Every cross-host capability Aegis tracks, with honest per-host status.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier, unique across the matrix.

description.description: What the capability is and why Aegis tracks it.

category.description: Grouping for the human-readable matrix. 'frontmatter' = skill/agent/command frontmatter fields; 'distribution' = install/packaging channels; 'runtime' = session-time behaviors; 'hooks' = lifecycle hooks; 'models' = model resolution; 'manifest' = plugin manifest fields; 'mcp' = Model Context Protocol; 'surface' = a whole surface type.

hosts.description: Per-host status. All five supported hosts are required.

status.description: supported = full native support; partial = shimmed or known-incomplete; gap = host has the concept but Aegis does not project it here; n/a = host has no concept of this capability at all.

evidence.description: A repo file path, a '<file>#<anchor>' or '<file>:<line>' reference, or a short note. REQUIRED when status is 'supported' or 'partial' (enforced by the F1 lint and by the conditional below).

since.description: Aegis release the status became true, e.g. 'v0.0.5'.
